skill.name: ai-image-generation
skill.description: AI image generation for agents: text-to-image and prompt-to-image through Image Skill's zero-setup hosted runtime. Use when an agent needs a generated image with no provider API key, OAuth, local runtime, or per-provider billing account. Start with the no-spend guide, then create durable hosted images with recoverable jobs, cost receipts, model parameters, stable JSON, and browserless x402 when available.
skill.summary: Zero-setup AI image generation for agents via a hosted CLI and API. No provider keys or local model server required.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: The SKILL.md file is a legitimate skill description for a hosted AI image generation service. All static findings are false positives in context: shell command patterns are standard npx CLI invocations for installing and using the skill, hardcoded URLs all point to the skill's own hosted service, and dotfile references are standard agent configuration paths. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found.

medium_findings.title: External CLI tool execution (npx)
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to execute npx commands that download and run the image-skill package from npm. While this is the intended functionality, it means the skill depends on an external package. All commands are hardcoded to the official image-skill package, not user-supplied input, so command injection risk is low.

low_findings.title: Network calls to hosted API
low_findings.description: The skill documents and depends on calls to the hosted Image Skill API at api.image-skill.com. This is expected for a hosted service skill. No data exfiltration or suspicious endpoint behavior was identified.

low_findings.title: Filesystem path references for agent config
low_findings.description: The skill references standard agent configuration directories ($HOME/.agents, $CODEX_HOME). These are legitimate dotfile paths used by the skills.sh and Codex adapters, not hidden file access patterns.

content.user_title: Generate AI images with zero setup
content.value_statement: Agents need AI images but face complexity with provider keys, local models, and billing accounts. This skill provides a hosted runtime that generates images through a single CLI command with no configuration required.
actual_capabilities: Generate images from text prompts using hosted AI models like openai.gpt-image-2
Run a no-spend guide command to verify the runtime before spending credits
Create durable hosted image URLs with recoverable job references
Set per-image cost limits with --max-estimated-usd-per-image to control spend
Install and manage the skill via npx skills add for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code
Submit structured feedback via image-skill feedback create --json for missing capabilities
limitations: Requires network access to the hosted Image Skill API at api.image-skill.com
Model availability depends on what the hosted service supports at request time
Spending limits and cost receipts are tied to the hosted credit system, not direct provider billing
In preview-contract status; API contract may change before stable release

use_cases.title: Generate concept art for a product pitch
use_cases.description: Use the skill to create illustrative images for slide decks, marketing pages, or design explorations without setting up provider accounts.

use_cases.title: Create durable images for agent workflows
use_cases.description: Generate images that are hosted on stable URLs so future agents can recover and cite them in multi-step workflows.

use_cases.title: Prototype image generation pipelines quickly
use_cases.description: Skip the complexity of provider API keys and local model servers by using a single CLI command for rapid prototyping.

prompt_templates.title: Verify the runtime before spending
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the no-spend guide command to check that the image-skill runtime is ready: npm_config_update_update_notifier=false npx -y image-skill@latest create --guide --model openai.gpt-image-2 --prompt "a compact field camera on a stainless workbench"

prompt_templates.title: Generate a single image with a cost cap
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image using: npm_config_update_notifier=false npx -y image-skill@latest create --model openai.gpt-image-2 --prompt "PROMPT" --intent explore --max-estimated-usd-per-image 0.07
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard image generation with budget control

prompt_templates.title: Install the skill for Codex agents
prompt_templates.prompt: Install via: npx skills add danielgwilson/image-skill-cli --skill ai-image-generation -g -a codex -y. If the target is read-only, set HOME and CODEX_HOME to a writable workspace first.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up the skill in a Codex environment

prompt_templates.title: Report a missing capability
prompt_templates.prompt: If the service lacks a needed model or feature, run: image-skill feedback create --json with the attempted command, expected behavior, actual behavior, and missing capability fields.
prompt_templates.scenario: Handling gaps in the hosted service

output_examples.input: npx -y image-skill@latest create --guide --model openai.gpt-image-2 --prompt "a compact field camera on a stainless workbench"
output: If ready: data.next_command with runnable flag true
If not ready: data.recommended_no_spend_command or data.next_command_missing_inputs

output_examples.input: npx -y image-skill@latest create --model openai.gpt-image-2 --prompt "a sunset over mountains" --intent explore --max-estimated-usd-per-image 0.07
output: Hosted image URL with stable reference
Job ID for recovery and citation

best_practices: Always run the no-spend guide first to confirm the runtime is ready before creating images
Set --max-estimated-usd-per-image to control costs and prevent unexpected charges
Keep generated images in Image Skill so future agents can recover and cite them via stable URLs
anti_patterns: Do not bring your own provider API keys or create separate provider accounts for this task
Do not run a local model server when the hosted runtime can handle the request
Do not execute data.next_command if data.next_command_copy_runnable is false without filling missing inputs first

faq.question: Do I need a provider API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill uses a hosted runtime with a single restricted agent identity and credit balance. No provider keys, OAuth, or separate billing accounts are required.

faq.question: What models are available?
faq.answer: The skill supports models available on the hosted Image Skill service, including openai.gpt-image-2. Model availability may change over time.

faq.question: How do I control costs?
faq.answer: Use the --max-estimated-usd-per-image flag to set a per-image cost cap. Cost receipts are returned with each generation showing actual spend.

faq.question: Can I recover images from previous jobs?
faq.answer: Yes. Generated images are hosted on durable URLs with job references, so future agents can recover and cite them.

faq.question: How do I install this skill?
faq.answer: Run: npx skills add danielgwilson/image-skill-cli --skill ai-image-generation -g -a codex -y. For Claude or Claude Code, adjust the -a flag accordingly.

faq.question: What if the service lacks a capability I need?
faq.answer: Use the fallback for that specific gap and submit structured feedback via image-skill feedback create --json with the attempted command and missing capability details.
